title: Argentine Corn Casserole
description: A smoky, flavorful corn casserole that's perfect for a sick day or a weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a large copper pot, cook the diced bacon over medium heat until crispy.
Remove the cooked bacon from the pot and set aside.
Add the diced onion to the pot and cook until translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the white cornmeal to the pot and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the heavy cream and eggs.
Add the whisked cream and egg mixture to the pot and stir until well combined.
Stir in the shredded smoked cheddar cheese and cooked bacon.
Pour the mixture into a 9x13 inch baking dish and bake for 35-40 minutes.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for 10-15 minutes before serving.
